lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Who are you?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Hi there. I'm Ektheme, an expert on the Xerician era.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who was Xeric?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Xeric was a dark mage who ruled Great Kourend for at least ten decades. A time period that is sometimes known as the Age of Strife.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: At least? Do you not know how long he was in power for?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: We do have records of course. However, while Xeric was indeed ruler of the kingdom from the 37th to the 47th decades, some believe that he actually ruled for longer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How come?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: It is often speculated that Xeric and his predecessor, King Shayzien VII, were in fact the same person.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How could that be?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Like Xeric, King Shayzien VII was fascinated with the Dark Altar.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: The King was killed at the Battle of the Concourse during the 37th decade. We have many accounts of this, yet his body was never recovered.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Just days after his death, we have the first recorded mention of Xeric, an incredibly powerful mage who resembled the King.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: It is written that Xeric killed the rulers of Kourend's five cities and claimed the kingdom as his own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So King Shayzien VII was resurrected as Xeric using the Dark Altar?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: It's possible. I am no expert on necromancy but we know the Dark Altar has the power to resurrect beasts. We also know that it can extend life. It's how the people of Arceuus are immortal.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: King Shayzien VII may have found a way to combine these powers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did Xeric have so much power?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Xeric drew much of his power from the Dark Altar, but he asserted his authority as ruler through his personal army of creatures, the lizardmen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Xeric created the lizardmen!?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Sadly so, though we're not exactly sure how he did so. Xeric used the citizens of Kourend for many experiments, as well as for his own sadistic pleasures.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: We have little written record of the experiments, but we believe they might have led to the creation of the lizardmen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know why he created the lizardmen?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Originally, we believed that Xeric created the lizardmen. However, recent discovieres suggest they were actually created during the time of King Shayzien VII.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know why Xeric needed the lizardmen?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Xeric was undoubtedly powerful, but he couldn't rule the people singlehandedly, nor could he rely on human informants. He needed an army he could control.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Lizardmen are loyal creatures and would do as he commanded without question. With them at his disposal, the people were powerless to resist his authority.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to the lizardmen after Xeric's reign?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: They remain loyal to Xeric. Even to this day they continue to attack the kingdom, as you may have seen.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Fortunately, the Shayzien Army keeps them under control.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do they keep fighting? Surely after a thousand years they must know Xeric is gone?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Without examining their minds, we will never know for sure. However, we assume Xeric genetically altered them to enforce that their duty is to serve him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But if they are serving Xeric, why do they attack the kingdom?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: A good question. One that I'm afraid I do not know the answer to.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Is there anything else you would like to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to Xeric?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: During the 47th decade, after ten decades of oppression, the people revolted against Xeric and his army of lizardmen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did the people suffer for so long?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: The events were triggered through the courage of one man, Byrne. He was not a man of particular stature of nobility, though he did proceed to become king.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: 'Twas the 312th day of decade 47. Byrne, a farmer by trade, is said to have witnessed a lizardman guard murder a child whilst working his farm.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Outraged at what he had just seen, Byrne grabbed his pitchfork and sprinted towards the guard.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Blinded by fury, Byrne attacked the guard, slaughtering the lizardman in plain sight of all around.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Legend has it Byrne then gave a galvanizing speech, standing over the butchered guard with his bloodied pitchfork held aloft in the air, urging the people to fight back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know what he said?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Sadly no. While we have many witness accounts, they all differ, so we can not verify it at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, so what happened next?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Whatever Byrne said, the people responded. Together, the citizens of Kourend revolted, grabbing whatever they could use as a weapon. They fought the lizardmen.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: The lizardmen, while strong and powerful, were simply outnumbered. In a matter of days, they had been driven back into the Kebos Lowlands.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what happened to Xeric?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: During the battle, the temple surrounding the Dark Altar was destroyed, weakening Xeric's power. We believe he fled to Quidamortem.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Some say Xeric was actually killed during the revolt, but we have no record of his remains being discovered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So no one knows what really happened to him?

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Xeric was incredibly powerful but we can reasonably assume that he soon perished. Without the power of the Dark Altar, Xeric would have become mortal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good riddance by the sounds of it.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Indeed. Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Xeric was incredibly powerful, but without the Dark Altar, he would have soon become mortal and perished.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: However, recent discoveries suggest that he may have found a way to amplify the power of the altar. This may have allowed him to live far longer than previously believed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm, interesting.

lines.speaker: Ektheme
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Goodbye.
